steps.title: Recognize the exponential decay per 5-second interval
steps.content:
Every 5 seconds, points decrease by 20%, meaning they are multiplied by $(1 - 0.20) = 0.80$.

Tracking the pattern:
  • At $x = 5$ (one 5-second interval): points become $490 \times 0.80$
  • At $x = 10$ (two intervals): points become $490 \times 0.80 \times 0.80 = 490 \times (0.80)^2$
  • At $x = 15$ (three intervals): points become $490 \times (0.80)^3$
In general:
$$490 \times (0.80)^{\text{(number of 5-second intervals)}}$$

steps.title: Express the exponent in terms of x
steps.content:
In $x$ seconds, there are exactly $\frac{x}{5}$ complete 5-second intervals.

Substituting into the exponential pattern:
$$f(x) = 490(0.80)^{\frac{x}{5}}$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
